title: Kalgrath
content:

{{Quote|I touched its mind enough to see it was howling in loneliness...|Luke Skywalker|<ref name="TNB" />}}
'''Kalgrath''', also known as the "'''Night Beast'''," was a [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warrior put into hibernation within the [[isolation chambers]] beneath the temples by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord of the Sith]] [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]], to guard [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]] against intruders prior to the [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Great Sith War)|Republic/Jedi assault]] on the moon.

==Biography==
===Original master===
When [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]] revealed the location of [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun's]] base on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]],<ref name="Sith War 6" /> the [[Republic Rim Fleet]]<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> [[Battle of Yavin 4 (Great Sith War)|arrived]] at the jungle moon. As the fleet arrived, Exar Kun watched from atop [[Great Temple/Legends|his temple]] and ordered Kalgrath to go to the [[isolation chambers]]<ref name="Sith War 6" /> [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] had built<ref name="DSS" /> beneath the temples.<ref name="Sith War 6" />

There, Exar Kun altered the mutated [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] warrior even further. He caused his tough hide to harden until it could block most conventional energy weapons, made his skin color go from crimson to deep green, to better camouflage in the catacombs and jungles of Yavin 4, and rendered Kalgrath mute.<ref name="DSS" /> Kalgrath was put in a deep sleep by Kun<ref name="TEGtAS" /> using [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith alchemy]]<ref name="DSS" /> and left behind there by the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] as a guardian.<ref name="TNEGtC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> Kalgrath would remain in hibernation for millennia<ref name="CSWE Night beast">{{CSWECite|II|379|night beast}}</ref> after most of his people were exterminated, and some fled into the parts unknown.<ref name="CSWE Massassi">{{CSWECite|II|292|Massassi}}</ref>

===Awakening===
[[File:KalgrathAttack-CSW9.png|thumb|left|220px|Kalgrath attacks Luke Skywalker and Han Solo]]
During an attack of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Amise Griff]],<ref name="CSWE Night beast" /> Kalgrath was awakened from his millennia-long sleep by a crashed [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE/sa bomber]] in the isolation chambers,<ref name="TNB" /> cracking his suspended-animation capsule open.<ref name="CSWE Night beast" /> He then went to look for his people believing they were still alive,<ref name="TEGtAS" /> not knowing the Massassi had been killed in Kun's mad bid for supernatural power.<ref name="DSS" /> Instead, Kalgrath discovered the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] base in the ancient temple. Thinking they were his ancient enemies, he attacked them. However, he was stopped by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]].<ref name="TNB" />

[[File:Nightbeast_swenc.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The Night Beast on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]].]]
Skywalker managed to communicate with Kalgrath through [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Kalgrath was able to calm himself down, allowing his insanity to fade. Luke agreed to help him to find his people. Kalgrath was put aboard a [[GR-75 medium transport/Legends|GR-75 medium transport]]<ref name="TNB" /> programmed with an auto-jump<ref name="CSWE Night beast" /><ref name="TNB" /> to the former location of the ancient [[Sith Empire/Legends|Sith Empire]]<ref name="TEGtAS" /><ref name="DSS" /> to find the remaining members of his people.<ref name="TEGtAS" /> One of the random jumps performed by the transport brought the beast back to Alliance space. This time, through the Force, Luke explained that the beast would eventually find its former masters by getting back onto the transport.<ref name="CSWE Night beast" />

===Legacy===
After Luke founded his Jedi academy, [[Tionne Solusar]] did some research into Kalgrath's fate, and have been able to track down the creature to the deserted world of [[Ziost/Legends|Ziost]] before the trail ended. She was informed by researchers on Ziost that they had seen a dark shadow creeping around their [[Unidentified camp|camp]].<ref name="TEGtAS" /> Despite this, it remains unknown whether the one-of-a-kind creature found his way to the fallen Sith Empire, with little more than rumours to back up Tionne's hypothesis.<ref name="DSS" />

Later, on the planet [[Seidhkona]], the [[Mind-witch]] [[S'ybll]] conjured up an illusion of Kalgrath from Luke Skywalker's memory. Skywalker fled from the illusion and into some ruins which collapsed on him, leaving him incapacitated.<ref name="TPD">"[[The Paradise Detour]]"</ref><ref name="TLoLS">''[[A New Hope: The Life of Luke Skywalker]]''</ref>

==Powers and abilities==
Following his alteration by Exar Kun,<ref name="DSS" /> the Night Beast could shield himself with the Force by instinct, making him invulnerable to energy weapons,<ref name="PotJS">''[[Power of the Jedi Sourcebook]]''</ref><ref name="DSS" /><ref name="CSWE Night beast" /> including blaster fire of all types and even [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] attacks.<ref name="TNB" />

==Behind the scenes==
Kalgrath, identified only as the "Night Beast," first appeared in ''[[The Night Beast]]'', a [[Star Wars (comic strip)|comic strip]] story arc written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and published in [[1982]].<ref name="TNB" /> The character was identified as Kalgrath in the [[2001]] reference book [[The Essential Guide to Alien Species (real-world book)|''The Essential Guide to Alien Species'']].<ref name="TEGtAS" /> The Night Beast resembles the [[Sentinel (creature)|Sentinel]], a creature that protected an [[Unidentified Alashan city|underground city]] on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Alashan]], as pictured in "[[The Guardian of Forever!]]" storyline in the [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (Marvel UK)|''Star Wars Weekly'']], written by [[Chris Claremont]], illustrated by [[Carmine Infantino]] and [[Gene Day]], and published in the [[Star Wars Weekly 113|''Star Wars Weekly'' 113]] on [[March 12]], [[1980]].<ref name="TGoF">{{StoryCite|story=The Guardian of Forever!|book=Star Wars Weekly 113}}</ref>
